name: ज्ञानविज्ञानयोग
meaning: The Yoga of Knowledge and Realisation
teaching: Krishna begins to speak of himself: the taste in water, the light in the moon and sun, the thread on which all worlds are strung like pearls. The divine is not elsewhere — it is the essence inside every essence. Few seek this; of the four kinds who turn to God, the one who seeks knowing itself is dearest, for that one seeks nothing in return. The Teacher turns the lamp on Himself: two natures, one thread through all pearls, a veil divine and crossable only by refuge. Four kinds of seekers arrive; the rarest says — Vasudeva is everything.
questions: When you pray or aspire, which of the four are you — the distressed, the seeker of gain, the curious, or the one who simply wants to know?
Can you taste water today and notice what the taste itself is?
What 'deity' — career, approval, security — do you worship for limited fruit?

verses.devanagari:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः ।
असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु॥
verses.transliteration:
mayyāsaktamanāḥ pārtha yogaṃ yuñjanmadāśrayaḥ
asaṃśayaṃ samagraṃ māṃ yathā jñāsyasi tacchṛṇu
verses.essence: With mind attached to me, practicing yoga with me as refuge — hear how you shall know me wholly, beyond doubt.
verses.translation: O Arjuna, hear how you shall without doubt know Me fully, with the mind intent on Me, practising Yoga and taking refuge in Me.

anvaya.meaning: with mind attached to me, O Partha
practicing yoga, with me as refuge
without doubt, completely, me
how you shall know — that hear

verses.explanation: A new movement opens: the first six chapters taught the self; the next six will teach the One the self meets. The conditions are intimacy itself — mind attached *to me*, refuge *in me* — and the promise is audacious: samagram, knowing the Infinite *wholly*, with doubt gone. Attachment, the great enemy so far, is rehabilitated when its object is the Divine.
verses.connection: Picks up exactly where 6.47 left the crown — with the most-yoked lover of God — and begins teaching what the beloved is.
verses.reflection: Practice today with mind attached to the highest and refuge actually taken — and expect to know, not guess.
verses.background: A new movement of the dialogue begins: Krishna turns from the discipline of the seeker to the nature of the One sought. Hear, he says, how with mind attached to Me and refuge in Me you shall know Me fully — the knowledge after which nothing remains to be known, attained by hardly one among thousands.

verses.devanagari:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः ।
यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
jñānaṃ te'haṃ savijñānamidaṃ vakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ
yajjñātvā neha bhūyo'nyajjñātavyamavaśiṣyate
verses.essence: I shall teach you this knowledge together with realization, knowing which nothing further remains here to be known.
verses.translation: I shall declare to thee in full this knowledge combined with realisation, after knowing which nothing more here remains to be known.

anvaya.meaning: knowledge, to you, I, together with realization
this I shall declare without remainder
knowing which, not here, further
anything else to be known remains

verses.explanation: Two knowings promised as a pair: jñāna, the understanding that can be taught, and vijñāna, the direct taste that can only be had. And then the largest claim in epistemology: after this, nothing else *needs* knowing — because to know the root is to implicitly hold every branch. All other knowledge is regional; this one is of the territory itself.
verses.connection: Sets the stakes for the chapter: not another doctrine, but the knowledge that retires the search.
verses.reflection: Seek the knowledge that ends seeking: ask today not for more facts but for the root of facts.
verses.background: A new movement of the dialogue begins: Krishna turns from the discipline of the seeker to the nature of the One sought. Hear, he says, how with mind attached to Me and refuge in Me you shall know Me fully — the knowledge after which nothing remains to be known, attained by hardly one among thousands.

verses.devanagari:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये ।
यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः॥
verses.transliteration:
manuṣyāṇāṃ sahasreṣu kaścidyatati siddhaye
yatatāmapi siddhānāṃ kaścinmāṃ vetti tattvataḥ
verses.essence: Among thousands of humans, scarcely one strives for perfection; and of those who strive and succeed, scarcely one knows me in truth.
verses.translation: Among thousands of men, one perchance strives for perfection; even among those successful strivers, only one perchance knows Me in essence.

anvaya.meaning: among thousands of humans
someone (rare) strives for perfection
even of the striving and the perfected
scarcely one knows me in truth

verses.explanation: A double filter, stated without flattery: rare is the one who even begins; rarer still, among the accomplished themselves, the one who knows *him* as he is. The arithmetic is not meant to discourage but to locate Arjuna: he is being handed, mid-battlefield, what the rarest seekers spend lifetimes approaching. Manushyanam sahasreshu — the funnel verse, and the tradition reads its arithmetic as encouragement wearing sternness: rarity is not exclusion. Kashchid yatati siddhaye — the striving itself already places one in the thousands' exception. Commentators note the doubled rarity — even among the perfected, few know tattvatah — to keep the knower humble and the beginner hopeful.
verses.connection: Prices the gift of 7.1–2 — and makes its free offering to Arjuna the more astonishing.
verses.reflection: Let the rarity sober you sweetly: among thousands, one seeks. You are reading this — continue.
verses.background: A new movement of the dialogue begins: Krishna turns from the discipline of the seeker to the nature of the One sought. Hear, he says, how with mind attached to Me and refuge in Me you shall know Me fully — the knowledge after which nothing remains to be known, attained by hardly one among thousands.

verses.devanagari:
भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।
अहंकार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा॥
verses.transliteration:
bhūmirāpo'nalo vāyuḥ khaṃ mano buddhireva ca
ahaṃkāra itīyaṃ me bhinnā prakṛtiraṣṭadhā
verses.essence: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, understanding and ego — this is my nature, divided eightfold.
verses.translation: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intellect and egoism thus is My Nature divided eightfold.

anvaya.meaning: earth, water, fire, air
ether, mind, and understanding
and ego — this, of mine
is the nature, divided eightfold

verses.explanation: The inventory of everything science can touch: five elements ascending in subtlety, then the three instruments of inner life — mind, intellect, the very sense of 'I'. All eight, including the ego that feels most like *me*, are classed as prakṛti: his equipment, his lower nature. The self one ordinarily identifies with is here itemized as machinery.
verses.connection: Begins the chapter's metaphysics: the visible and mental world catalogued as the lower of two natures.
verses.reflection: List your own eightfold kit today — body, mind, intellect, ego — and note: equipment, not owner.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् ।
जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत्॥
verses.transliteration:
apareyamitastvanyāṃ prakṛtiṃ viddhi me parām
jīvabhūtāṃ mahābāho yayedaṃ dhāryate jagat
verses.essence: This is the lower. Know my other, higher nature, Mahabaho — the life-element by which this universe is upheld.
verses.translation: This is the inferior Prakriti, O mighty-armed (Arjuna); know thou as different from it My higher Prakriti (Nature), the very life-element, by which this world is upheld.

anvaya.meaning: this is the lower; but other than this
know my higher prakriti
which has become the jiva, O mighty-armed
by which this universe is sustained

verses.explanation: The hierarchy revealed: everything in 7.4 — galaxies, thoughts, the ego itself — is aparā, lower; above it stands the parā prakṛti, the conscious life-principle, jīva-bhūtā, which has become every living soul. Matter does not hold itself up; consciousness dhāryate, carries it. Each living being is a strand of the Divine's *higher* nature — the world's true infrastructure.
verses.connection: Completes 7.4's picture: two natures, one Lord — and the reader belongs to the higher one.
verses.schools_note: The 'higher prakriti, become the jiva' is a watershed: Shankara reads para prakriti as the conscious power of the one Self; Ramanuja builds his system on it — souls as a real, eternal category, the Lord's higher nature, distinct from matter forever; the Gaudiyas call it the tatastha-shakti, the marginal energy. Whatever the school, the verse settles one thing: consciousness is never a product of the lower eight.
verses.reflection: Feel the higher nature in you this hour: the aliveness that uses the equipment of 7.4.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय ।
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा॥
verses.transliteration:
etadyonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇītyupadhāraya
ahaṃ kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayastathā
verses.essence: Know all beings to have these two as their womb. I am the origin of the entire universe, and likewise its dissolution.
verses.translation: Know that these two (Natures) are the womb of all beings. So I am the source and dissolution of the whole universe.

anvaya.meaning: beings having these two as womb
all — thus understand
I, of the entire universe
the origin, and likewise the dissolution

verses.explanation: The two natures meet, and everything alive is their child: matter the mother-stuff, conscious life the animating father-seed, both belonging to one owner. So the conclusion follows like arithmetic: prabhavaḥ pralayaḥ — I am where all this comes from and where it folds back. Source and sea, both him.
verses.connection: Joins 7.4–5 into a single cosmology with one proprietor at both ends.
verses.reflection: Trace one thing today to its double origin — matter shaped, consciousness witnessing — and bow to both.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय ।
मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव॥
verses.transliteration:
mattaḥ parataraṃ nānyatkiñcidasti dhanañjaya
mayi sarvamidaṃ protaṃ sūtre maṇigaṇā iva
verses.essence: There is nothing whatever higher than me, Dhananjaya. All this is strung on me like pearls on a thread.
verses.translation: There is nothing whatsoever higher than Me, O Arjuna. All this is strung on Me, as clusters of gems on a string.

anvaya.meaning: than me, higher, nothing else
whatever exists, O Dhananjaya
on me all this is strung
like rows of pearls on a thread

verses.explanation: First the flat absolute — mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat, nothing above me — then the image that has carried it for millennia: pearls on a thread. The thread runs through every pearl, unseen, giving the scattered beads their unity and their hang; remove it and the necklace is a handful of debris. The world's coherence *is* the hidden God within it. Mattah parataram nanyat — nothing whatever higher than Me: the Gita's flattest statement of supremacy, and then the thread-and-pearls image that softens it into intimacy. The schools read the sutra differently — Advaita as the substrate of appearance, Vishishtadvaita as the soul of the world-body — but all wear the necklace: whatever you handle, you are handling something strung on God.
verses.connection: Crowns 7.6's cosmology with the chapter's master-image — immanence as the invisible string.
verses.reflection: Look along one ordinary object today until you sense the thread it is strung on.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः ।
प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु॥
verses.transliteration:
raso'hamapsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śaśisūryayoḥ
praṇavaḥ sarvavedeṣu śabdaḥ khe pauruṣaṃ nṛṣu
verses.essence: I am the taste in water, Kaunteya, the radiance of moon and sun, the Om in all the Vedas, the sound in ether, the prowess in men.
verses.translation: I am the sapidity in water, O Arjuna; I am the light in the moon and the sun; I am the syllable Om in all the Vedas, sound in ether and virility in men.

anvaya.meaning: I am the taste in water, O Kaunteya
I am the radiance of moon and sun
the Om in all the Vedas
sound in ether, manliness in men

verses.explanation: The thread of 7.7 located, sense by sense: in each thing, its essence — not water but its taste, not the sun but its shining, not scripture but its seed-syllable. The hunt for God is redirected from elsewhere to *deeper here*: take any experience, find what makes it most itself, and you are touching the string.
verses.connection: Begins the immanence litany (7.8–11): where exactly the pearls touch the thread.
verses.reflection: Taste water once today as taste itself — the nearest daily sacrament, hiding in the glass.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ ।
जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु॥
verses.transliteration:
puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛthivyāṃ ca tejaścāsmi vibhāvasau
jīvanaṃ sarvabhūteṣu tapaścāsmi tapasviṣu
verses.essence: I am the pure fragrance in earth and the brilliance in fire, the life in all beings, and the austerity in ascetics.
verses.translation: I am the sweet fragrance in the earth and the brilliance in the fire, the life in all beings, and I am the austerity in ascetics.

anvaya.meaning: and the pure fragrance in the earth
and I am the brilliance in fire
the life in all beings
and the austerity in ascetics

verses.explanation: The litany deepens from qualities to powers: the scent that rises from rained-on earth, fire's heat-light, and then two that stop the breath — jīvanam, the sheer aliveness in everything alive, and tapas, the very intensity of the striver's striving. Even the effort to reach him is made of him.
verses.connection: Continues 7.8: the seeker discovers the sought inside the seeking itself.
verses.reflection: Catch the fragrance of earth, the heat of fire, your own aliveness — and address each one correctly.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् ।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
bījaṃ māṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ viddhi pārtha sanātanam
buddhirbuddhimatāmasmi tejastejasvināmaham
verses.essence: Know me, Partha, as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the intelligence of the intelligent, the splendor of the splendid.
verses.translation: Know Me, O Arjuna, as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the intelligence of the intelligent; the splendour of the splendid objects am I.

anvaya.meaning: me, the seed of all beings
know, O Partha, eternal
I am the intelligence of the intelligent
the splendor of the splendid, I

verses.explanation: Bīja sanātanam — the seed that is eternal: not one ancestor among others but the unspent origin present in every generation. Then the pattern sharpens into a formula the chapter will repeat: of any excellence, he is the excellence itself — the brightness *of* the bright, the sharpness *of* the sharp mind reading this.
verses.connection: Extends the litany from elements to capacities — God as the genius inside genius.
verses.reflection: When you meet brilliance today, in yourself or another, silently return the loan to its source.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम् ।
धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ॥
verses.transliteration:
balaṃ balavatāṃ cāhaṃ kāmarāgavivarjitam
dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu kāmo'smi bharatarṣabha
verses.essence: I am the strength of the strong, free of craving and passion; in beings I am desire not opposed to dharma, Bharatarshabha.
verses.translation: Of the strong, I am the strength devoid of desire and attachment, and in (all) beings, I am the desire unopposed to Dharma, O Arjuna.

anvaya.meaning: and I am the strength of the strong
devoid of craving and passion
not opposed to dharma, in beings
I am desire, O bull of the Bharatas

verses.explanation: The litany's most surprising line: kāma, desire itself — the enemy of chapter 3 — claimed by God, with one qualifier: dharmāviruddha, aligned with dharma. The longing to build, to love, to create within the bounds of rightness is not a tolerated weakness but a divine presence. The Gita sanctifies wanting; it only disowns wanting gone feral.
verses.connection: Completes 7.8–11 by reclaiming even desire — the chapter's quiet rehabilitation of the human.
verses.reflection: Honor one desire today that runs within dharma — wanting rightly is also His face.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये ।
मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि॥
verses.transliteration:
ye caiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasāstāmasāśca ye
matta eveti tānviddhi na tvahaṃ teṣu te mayi
verses.essence: Whatever states there are — of sattva, of rajas, of tamas — know them as from me alone. I am not in them; they are in me.
verses.translation: Whatever beings (and objects) that are pure, active and inert, know that they proceed from Me. They are in Me, yet I am not in them.

anvaya.meaning: and whatever sattvic states
and whatever rajasic and tamasic
know them as from me alone

anvaya.word: न तु अहं तेषु ते मयि
anvaya.meaning: yet I am not in them — they are in me

verses.explanation: Total sourcing with a precise asymmetry: every mood and mode the three gunas weave — luminous, driven, dull — issues from him; yet he is not *contained* in them, while they are contained in him. The ocean is in every wave; no wave holds the ocean. The formula prevents both errors: a God absent from the world, and a God reducible to it.
verses.connection: Wraps the immanence verses with their safeguard — presence without imprisonment.
verses.reflection: Whatever state arises in you today — bright, restless, dull — file it: from Him, contained in Him.
verses.background: Krishna discloses his anatomy of everything: the eightfold lower nature and the higher life-principle that sustains the universe; Himself the origin and dissolution of all, the thread on which worlds are strung like pearls — then the litany of immanence: taste in water, light in sun and moon, the seed eternal in all beings.

verses.devanagari:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत् ।
मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम्॥
verses.transliteration:
tribhirguṇamayairbhāvairebhiḥ sarvamidaṃ jagat
mohitaṃ nābhijānāti māmebhyaḥ paramavyayam
verses.essence: Deluded by these three states made of the gunas, this whole world does not recognize me, who am beyond them and imperishable.
verses.translation: Deluded by these Natures (states or things) composed of the three alities of Nature all this world does not know Me as distinct from them and immutable.

anvaya.meaning: by these three guna-made states
this whole world
deluded, does not recognize
me, beyond them, imperishable

verses.explanation: The diagnosis of the world's blindness: the gunas are a weather so continuous it is mistaken for the sky. Attention, captured by the three-colored play, never asks what the play is projected on. He is not hidden far away but hidden in plain depth — param, just beyond the moods, exactly where no one looks.
verses.connection: Explains 7.3's rarity: the veil is woven of the very states everyone lives inside.
verses.reflection: Notice the lens at work once today: the gunas showing you everything except what stands behind them.
verses.background: The teaching confronts the veil: deluded by the three strands, the world does not recognize Him; His divine maya is hard to cross — but those who take refuge in Him cross it. Four kinds of virtuous seekers come; dearest is the knower; and rarest, after many births, the soul that says Vasudeva is all.

verses.devanagari:
दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया ।
मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते॥
verses.transliteration:
daivī hyeṣā guṇamayī mama māyā duratyayā
māmeva ye prapadyante māyāmetāṃ taranti te
verses.essence: For this divine maya of mine, made of the gunas, is hard to cross. Those who take refuge in me alone pass beyond this maya.
verses.translation: Verily, this divine illusion of Mine, made up of the (three) alities (of Nature) is difficult to cross over; those who take refuge in Me alone, cross over this illusion.

anvaya.meaning: for divine is this guna-made
maya of mine, hard to cross
those who take refuge in me alone
they cross over this maya

verses.explanation: The veil of 7.13 is named and given an owner: mama māyā — *my* maya, daivī, divine, not a cosmic accident but his own power of enchantment. Hence the futility of fighting it head-on: duratyayā, no technique unaided storms the magician's spell. The single exit is stated with surgical economy: mām eva ye prapadyante — go past the spell by going to the one who casts it. Daivi hy esha guna-mayi — the maya verse the whole later tradition orbits. Mama maya: the veil is His, divine workmanship, not a rival power — which is exactly why mam eva ye prapadyante works: the weaver can part what no one can tear. Commentators across schools converge on the verse's practical core: the exit from illusion is not analysis but allegiance.
verses.connection: Answers 7.13's predicament: the door through the veil is surrender to its weaver.
verses.schools_note: A watershed verse. Advaita reads maya as the inexplicable power of appearance that knowledge dispels; the bhakti schools stress the verse's own remedy — prapatti, refuge — making grace, not analysis, the crossing. All agree on its force: self-effort alone does not break this enchantment.
verses.reflection: When the veil proves uncrossable today, stop climbing and try the door: refuge, simply taken.
verses.background: The teaching confronts the veil: deluded by the three strands, the world does not recognize Him; His divine maya is hard to cross — but those who take refuge in Him cross it. Four kinds of virtuous seekers come; dearest is the knower; and rarest, after many births, the soul that says Vasudeva is all.

verses.devanagari:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः ।
माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः॥
verses.transliteration:
na māṃ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ
māyayāpahṛtajñānā āsuraṃ bhāvamāśritāḥ
verses.essence: Evil-doers, the deluded, the lowest of men do not take refuge in me — their discernment stolen by maya, clinging to a demonic mode of being.
verses.translation: The evil-doers and the deluded who are the lowest of men do not seek Me; they whose knowledge is destroyed by illusion follow the ways of demons.

anvaya.meaning: not to me do the evil-doers, the deluded
take refuge — the lowest of men
their knowledge carried off by maya
resorting to the asuric mode of being

verses.explanation: If the door is open and simple, why is it not crowded? Four declines, really one: māyayāpahṛta-jñānāḥ — the very faculty that would seek the door has been pickpocketed by the enchantment. The āsura mode is not horns and fangs; it is the settled conviction that grabbing is wiser than offering, which makes surrender look like the one absurd move.
verses.connection: The shadow side of 7.14's promise: who declines the crossing, and what declined in them first.
verses.reflection: Check where cleverness in you serves the wrong master — intelligence stolen is the subtlest theft.
verses.background: The teaching confronts the veil: deluded by the three strands, the world does not recognize Him; His divine maya is hard to cross — but those who take refuge in Him cross it. Four kinds of virtuous seekers come; dearest is the knower; and rarest, after many births, the soul that says Vasudeva is all.

verses.devanagari:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन ।
आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ॥
verses.transliteration:
caturvidhā bhajante māṃ janāḥ sukṛtino'rjuna
ārto jijñāsurarthārthī jñānī ca bharatarṣabha
verses.essence: Four kinds of virtuous people worship me, Arjuna: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of wealth, and the knower.
verses.translation: Four kinds of virtuous men worship Me, O Arjuna, and they are the distressed, the seekr of knowledge, the seekr of wealth and the wise, O lord of the Bharatas.

anvaya.meaning: four kinds worship me
virtuous people, O Arjuna
the afflicted, the inquirer, the wealth-seeker
and the knower, O bull of the Bharatas

verses.explanation: A census of the world's prayers, conducted without contempt: the ārta who comes bleeding, the jijñāsu who comes wondering, the arthārthī who comes wanting — and the jñānī who comes for nothing, having found. All four are sukṛtinaḥ, *virtuous*: even the bargaining prayer turned toward him is credited as turning. Doors are graded by how far they open, not condemned for being doors. Chatur-vidha bhajante mam — the four-fold census of seekers: the hurting, the curious, the wanting, the knowing — all titled sukritinah, virtuous. The tradition reads the list as the Gita's pastoral genius: motives are graded later (7.17), but admitted now. Whatever first turns the face counts; the door does not interview the knock.
verses.connection: Maps who does walk through 7.14's door — every motive admitted at its own depth.
verses.reflection: Whichever of the four you are today — hurting, curious, needing, knowing — come anyway. All four count.
verses.background: The teaching confronts the veil: deluded by the three strands, the world does not recognize Him; His divine maya is hard to cross — but those who take refuge in Him cross it. Four kinds of virtuous seekers come; dearest is the knower; and rarest, after many births, the soul that says Vasudeva is all.

verses.devanagari:
तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते ।
प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः॥
verses.transliteration:
teṣāṃ jñānī nityayukta ekabhaktirviśiṣyate
priyo hi jñānino'tyarthamahaṃ sa ca mama priyaḥ
verses.essence: Of these, the knower — ever harnessed, devoted to the One alone — excels. For I am exceedingly dear to the knower, and the knower is dear to me.
verses.translation: Of them the wise, ever steadfast and devoted to the One, excels (is the best); for I am exceedingly dear to the wise and he is dear to Me.

anvaya.meaning: of these, the knower, ever-yoked
of single devotion, excels
for exceedingly dear, to the knower
am I, and he is dear to me

verses.explanation: The ranking, and its reason is not merit but love: eka-bhakti, devotion with one object only, because the jñānī has nothing left to ask the Divine *for* — the Divine has become the point. And then the line that humanizes the Absolute: priyo hi... aham, sa ca mama priyaḥ — I am dear to him; he is dear to *me*. The current runs both ways.
verses.connection: Crowns 7.16's four: when need falls away, what remains between soul and God is affection.
verses.reflection: Love the highest today for no reason at all — drop one 'because' from your devotion.
verses.background: The teaching confronts the veil: deluded by the three strands, the world does not recognize Him; His divine maya is hard to cross — but those who take refuge in Him cross it. Four kinds of virtuous seekers come; dearest is the knower; and rarest, after many births, the soul that says Vasudeva is all.

verses.devanagari:
उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् ।
आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम्॥
verses.transliteration:
udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tvātmaiva me matam
āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā māmevānuttamāṃ gatim
verses.essence: Noble are they all — but the knower I hold to be my very Self. For that one, harnessed, abides in me as the highest goal.
verses.translation: Noble indeed are all these; but I deem the wise man as My very Self; for, steadfast in mind he is established in Me alone as the supreme goal.

anvaya.meaning: noble indeed are all these

anvaya.word: ज्ञानी तु आत्मा एव मे मतम्
anvaya.meaning: but the knower is my very Self — so I hold
for that one, with self yoked, abides
in me alone as the unsurpassed goal

verses.explanation: First the courtesy — udārāḥ sarva eva, all four are noble, none dismissed — then the staggering elevation: ātmaiva me, the knower is *my own Self*. Not servant, not even friend: the separation itself has worn through. When a being wants nothing but God, God ceases to count that being as other.
verses.connection: Pushes 7.17's mutual love to identity — the chapter's quiet peak of intimacy.
verses.reflection: Hear the staggering courtesy once more: the knower is His very self. Aspire to be so absorbed.
verses.background: The teaching confronts the veil: deluded by the three strands, the world does not recognize Him; His divine maya is hard to cross — but those who take refuge in Him cross it. Four kinds of virtuous seekers come; dearest is the knower; and rarest, after many births, the soul that says Vasudeva is all.

verses.devanagari:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते ।
वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः॥
verses.transliteration:
bahūnāṃ janmanāmante jñānavānmāṃ prapadyate
vāsudevaḥ sarvamiti sa mahātmā sudurlabhaḥ
verses.essence: At the end of many births, the one of knowledge takes refuge in me, realizing 'Vasudeva is all there is.' Such a great soul is exceedingly rare.
verses.translation: At the end of many births the wise man comes to Me, realising that all this is Vaasudeva (the innermost Self); such a great soul (Mahatma) is very hard to find.

anvaya.meaning: at the end of many births
the one possessed of knowledge takes refuge in me
'Vasudeva is everything' — thus (realizing)
that great soul is very hard to find

verses.explanation: The timeline of the jñānī's arrival: bahūnāṁ janmanām ante — lifetimes of seeking, refining, exhausting every partial answer, until the recognition that cannot be taught dawns whole: vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti, *this* — all of it — is He. Three words that end the search because they leave nowhere outside Him to search. Su-durlabhaḥ: the universe does not produce this often. Vasudevah sarvam iti — the mahavakya of the Gita's own minting: Vasudeva is all there is. Bahunam janmanam ante — the price tag is lifetimes, and the commentaries refuse to discount it: this sentence is not adopted but arrived at. Sa mahatma su-durlabhah: Chaitanya's tradition reads the verse weeping; Shankara reads it as knowledge's final form. Both call it the rarest sentence a human ever truly says.
verses.connection: Gives 7.17–18's knower his biography — and the mantra in which all knowing terminates.
verses.schools_note: 'Vasudevah sarvam iti' is claimed by every school as its own summit: for Advaita, the realization of non-duality itself; for Vaishnavas, the vision that the personal Krishna pervades and owns all. The verse holds both readings without strain — which is perhaps its point.
verses.reflection: Practice the rare sentence once today, looking at anything: Vasudeva is all this.
verses.background: The teaching confronts the veil: deluded by the three strands, the world does not recognize Him; His divine maya is hard to cross — but those who take refuge in Him cross it. Four kinds of virtuous seekers come; dearest is the knower; and rarest, after many births, the soul that says Vasudeva is all.

verses.devanagari:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः ।
तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया॥
verses.transliteration:
kāmaistaistairhṛtajñānāḥ prapadyante'nyadevatāḥ
taṃ taṃ niyamamāsthāya prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā
verses.essence: Those whose discernment is carried off by this or that desire resort to other deities, following this or that rite, constrained by their own natures.
verses.translation: Those whose wisdom has been rent away by this or that desire, go to other gods, following this or that rite, led by their own nature.

anvaya.meaning: by various desires, their knowledge carried away
they resort to other deities
adopting this or that observance
constrained by their own nature

verses.explanation: The mirror of 7.19: where the knower's vision gathers everything into One, desire splinters vision into many — each want hiring the deity of its department. The verdict is diagnostic, not sneering: prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā, steered by their own natures. People worship at the altitude their longings allow.
verses.connection: Returns from the rare summit to the common landscape: why most worship remains plural and transactional.
verses.reflection: Audit your altars honestly: which of your worships were designed by your appetites?
verses.background: Krishna accounts for the world's many altars: desires carry people to other gods, and He Himself steadies each one's faith and grants the fruits through those very forms — but finite worship wins finite goods; the worshipers of gods go to gods; His own come to Him.

verses.devanagari:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति ।
तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
yo yo yāṃ yāṃ tanuṃ bhaktaḥ śraddhayārcitumicchati
tasya tasyācalāṃ śraddhāṃ tāmeva vidadhāmyaham
verses.essence: Whatever form a devotee seeks to worship with faith, that very faith I make firm and unwavering.
verses.translation: Whatsoever form any devotee desires to worship with faith that (same) faith of his I make firm and unflinching.

anvaya.word: यः यः यां यां तनुं भक्तः
anvaya.meaning: whatever devotee, whatever form
desires to worship with faith
of that very one, the unwavering faith
that indeed I bestow

verses.explanation: A theological astonishment hiding in administrative language: when anyone, anywhere, kindles faith toward any form — that flame is steadied *by him*. He underwrites the devotions of his apparent rivals; the God people bypass is the one keeping their bypass lit. Generosity at this depth has stopped keeping score.
verses.connection: Deepens 4.11's 'as they approach me' — even the detour's fuel is supplied by the destination.
verses.schools_note: The Lord steadying faith aimed at other deities is the tradition's test-case in theology of tolerance: Shankara — all forms are the one Self, so all faith is ultimately well-placed; Ramanuja — the Lord, as inner Self of every deva, receives and rewards the worship Himself (cited with 7.22-23's finitude clause); the verse thus grounds both generosity toward all worship and the recommendation of its highest address.
verses.reflection: Respect every sincere faith you meet today — the steadiness in it is underwritten from above.
verses.background: Krishna accounts for the world's many altars: desires carry people to other gods, and He Himself steadies each one's faith and grants the fruits through those very forms — but finite worship wins finite goods; the worshipers of gods go to gods; His own come to Him.

verses.devanagari:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते ।
लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान्॥
verses.transliteration:
sa tayā śraddhayā yuktastasyārādhanamīhate
labhate ca tataḥ kāmānmayaivavihitānhi tān
verses.essence: Endowed with that faith, one worships that form and obtains one's desires from it — though in truth they are dispensed by me alone.
verses.translation: Endowed with that faith, he engages in the worship of that (form) and from it he obtains his desire, these being verily ordained by Me (alone).

anvaya.meaning: that one, endowed with that faith
strives in the worship of that (form)
and obtains thence the desires
which are ordained, in truth, by me alone

verses.explanation: The supply chain behind every answered prayer: the worship goes to the chosen counter, the request is filled — mayaiva vihitān, from his warehouse, always. No deity is an independent vendor; all granting routes through the one Granter. The system works precisely because its many faces share a single treasury.
verses.connection: Completes 7.21's mechanics: faith steadied by him, fruits delivered by him — the middle gods are windows.
verses.reflection: Receive one answered wish today knowing who the real treasurer was.
verses.background: Krishna accounts for the world's many altars: desires carry people to other gods, and He Himself steadies each one's faith and grants the fruits through those very forms — but finite worship wins finite goods; the worshipers of gods go to gods; His own come to Him.

verses.devanagari:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् ।
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि॥
verses.transliteration:
antavattu phalaṃ teṣāṃ tadbhavatyalpamedhasām
devāndevayajo yānti madbhaktā yānti māmapi
verses.essence: But finite is the fruit of those of small understanding. Worshipers of the gods go to the gods; my devotees come to me.
verses.translation: Verily the reward (fruit) that accrues to those men of small intelligence is finite. The worshippers of the gods go to them, but My devotees come to Me.

anvaya.meaning: but ending-bound is the fruit of those
it becomes, of the small-minded
to the gods go the god-worshipers
my devotees come even to me

verses.explanation: The honest price-tag on 7.21–22's generosity: antavat, the lesser counters pay in perishable coin — health, wealth, heavens with expiry dates. The law beneath it is destination-by-orientation: you arrive at what you aimed at. Aim at the limited and receive it, fully; aim at the Unlimited and receive *that*. The tragedy of small prayers is not that they fail but that they succeed.
verses.connection: The fine print after the open-handedness: every worship works, and each delivers exactly its own size.
verses.reflection: Aim today at the source, not the perks — finite worship buys finite goods.
verses.background: Krishna accounts for the world's many altars: desires carry people to other gods, and He Himself steadies each one's faith and grants the fruits through those very forms — but finite worship wins finite goods; the worshipers of gods go to gods; His own come to Him.

verses.devanagari:
अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः ।
परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम्॥
verses.transliteration:
avyaktaṃ vyaktimāpannaṃ manyante māmabuddhayaḥ
paraṃ bhāvamajānanto mamāvyayamanuttamam
verses.essence: The unintelligent think of me, the unmanifest, as having come into manifestation — not knowing my higher being, imperishable and supreme.
verses.translation: The foolish think of Me, the Unmanifest, as having manifestation, knowing not My higher, immutable and most excellent nature.

anvaya.meaning: the unmanifest as having entered manifestation
think me, the undiscerning
not knowing my higher being
mine — imperishable, unsurpassed

verses.explanation: The subtlest error in the chapter: taking the visible Krishna as the whole of Krishna — a man who became briefly divine, or a god shrunk to fit a body. The correction: the form is real, but behind it stands paraṁ bhāvam, an unmanifest supremacy the eyes never exhaust. The error is not seeing him; it is concluding that what is seen is all there is.
verses.connection: Guards 7.19's 'Vasudeva is all' from its cheap inversion — 'all of Vasudeva is this figure'.
verses.reflection: Catch yourself shrinking the infinite to its visible form today — honor the door, seek the dwelling.
verses.background: The chapter closes on the hiddenness and the disclosure: the unintelligent take the unmanifest to have become merely visible; veiled by yoga-maya He is not revealed to all; He knows all beings, none know Him — yet those freed of the delusion of opposites worship Him firmly and know Him even at the hour of departure.

verses.devanagari:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः ।
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम्॥
verses.transliteration:
nāhaṃ prakāśaḥ sarvasya yogamāyāsamāvṛtaḥ
mūḍho'yaṃ nābhijānāti loko māmajamavyayam
verses.essence: Veiled by my yoga-maya, I am not revealed to all. This deluded world does not recognize me — unborn, imperishable.
verses.translation: I am not manifest to all (as I am) veiled by the Yoga-Maya. This deluded world does not know Me, the unborn and imperishable.

anvaya.meaning: I am not manifest to everyone
enveloped by yoga-maya
this deluded (world) does not recognize
me — the world — as unborn, undecaying

verses.explanation: Why the error of 7.24 is nearly universal: the concealment is active. Yoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥ — wrapped in his own power of seeming, the way an actor's role hides the actor most perfectly from those nearest the stage. The hiddenness of God is not distance or absence; it is camouflage worn within the scene.
verses.connection: Explains the veil's operation: 7.13's blindness is also, from his side, a deliberate discretion.
verses.reflection: Let the divine hiddenness be courtesy, not absence — room left for your love to choose freely.
verses.background: The chapter closes on the hiddenness and the disclosure: the unintelligent take the unmanifest to have become merely visible; veiled by yoga-maya He is not revealed to all; He knows all beings, none know Him — yet those freed of the delusion of opposites worship Him firmly and know Him even at the hour of departure.

verses.devanagari:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन ।
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन॥
verses.transliteration:
vedāhaṃ samatītāni vartamānāni cārjuna
bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni māṃ tu veda na kaścana
verses.essence: I know the beings of the past, the present and the future, Arjuna — but no one knows me.
verses.translation: I know, O Arjuna, the beings of the past, the present and the future, but no one knows Me.

anvaya.meaning: I know those gone before
and the present ones, O Arjuna
and the beings to come
but me, no one knows

verses.explanation: The asymmetry of knowledge, drawn absolutely: every being across all three times stands transparent before him; he stands opaque before every being. The mirror sees the room; the room cannot see into the mirror. Said by anyone else, vanity — said from the position 7.24–25 described, simple geometry.
verses.connection: Caps the hiddenness verses with their starkest formulation — total knower, totally unknown.
verses.reflection: Rest in the asymmetry today: fully known by what you cannot corner. Let yourself be the known one.
verses.background: The chapter closes on the hiddenness and the disclosure: the unintelligent take the unmanifest to have become merely visible; veiled by yoga-maya He is not revealed to all; He knows all beings, none know Him — yet those freed of the delusion of opposites worship Him firmly and know Him even at the hour of departure.

verses.devanagari:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत ।
सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप॥
verses.transliteration:
icchādveṣasamutthena dvandvamohena bhārata
sarvabhūtāni sammohaṃ sarge yānti parantapa
verses.essence: By the delusion of the pairs arising from desire and aversion, Bharata, all beings walk into bewilderment at birth itself.
verses.translation: By the delusion of the pairs of opposites arising from desire and aversion, O Bharata, all beings are subject to delusion at birth, O Parantapa.

anvaya.meaning: arisen from desire and aversion
by the delusion of the pairs, O Bharata
all beings into bewilderment
go, at creation itself, O Parantapa

verses.explanation: The mechanics of the universal blindness: icchā and dveṣa — want and recoil — generate the pairs, and the pairs (pleasant-unpleasant, gain-loss, mine-yours) form the lens every newborn perceives through, sarge, from the very start. No one *falls* into delusion; everyone arrives pre-fitted with it. Which makes the next verse's exit all the more precious.
verses.connection: Traces 7.25–26's unknowing to its engine: the sorting reflex installed before the first memory.
verses.reflection: Watch the pull-push trance for one hour — every like and dislike a thread of the birth-veil.
verses.background: The chapter closes on the hiddenness and the disclosure: the unintelligent take the unmanifest to have become merely visible; veiled by yoga-maya He is not revealed to all; He knows all beings, none know Him — yet those freed of the delusion of opposites worship Him firmly and know Him even at the hour of departure.

verses.devanagari:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् ।
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः॥
verses.transliteration:
yeṣāṃ tvantagataṃ pāpaṃ janānāṃ puṇyakarmaṇām
te dvandvamohanirmuktā bhajante māṃ dṛḍhavratāḥ
verses.essence: But those whose wrongdoing has come to an end, people of pure deeds — freed from the delusion of the pairs, they worship me with firm resolve.
verses.translation: But those men of virtuous deeds whose sins have come to an end, and who are freed from the delusion of the pairs of opposites, worship Me, steadfast in their vows.

anvaya.meaning: but those whose sin has reached its end
of people of meritorious deeds
they, released from the pairs' delusion
worship me, firm in vow

verses.explanation: The exit from 7.27's congenital fog, and its sequence matters: anta-gataṁ pāpam — accumulated wrong worn down by lives of clean action — *then* the lens of the pairs loosens, *then* worship becomes dṛḍha-vrata, steady. Purity is not the reward of vision; it is vision's prerequisite. Conduct clears the windshield that doctrine alone cannot.
verses.connection: Answers 7.27: the fog is congenital but not permanent — washed thin by lived goodness.
verses.reflection: Do one clean deed today and feel it thin the lens — ethics is optics here.
verses.background: The chapter closes on the hiddenness and the disclosure: the unintelligent take the unmanifest to have become merely visible; veiled by yoga-maya He is not revealed to all; He knows all beings, none know Him — yet those freed of the delusion of opposites worship Him firmly and know Him even at the hour of departure.

verses.devanagari:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये ।
ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम्॥
verses.transliteration:
jarāmaraṇamokṣāya māmāśritya yatanti ye
te brahma tadviduḥ kṛtsnamadhyātmaṃ karma cākhilam
verses.essence: Those who strive for freedom from old age and death, taking refuge in me — they know Brahman fully, the inmost self, and action entire.
verses.translation: Those who strive for liberation from old age and death, taking refuge in Me, realise in full ï1thatï1 Brahman, the whole knowledge of the Self and all action.

anvaya.meaning: for liberation from old age and death
taking refuge in me, those who strive
they know that Brahman entirely
the inmost self, and action complete

verses.explanation: The chapter begins its handover: those who flee the two unanswerables — aging and death — by fleeing *to him* are promised the whole curriculum: Brahman, adhyātma, karma, kṛtsnam, complete. Refuge turns out to be the fastest scholarship; what the mind chases for lifetimes, surrender is simply handed.
verses.connection: Opens the bridge to chapter 8 by naming the terms Arjuna is about to ask him to define.
verses.reflection: Let even the plain wish not to be erased drive your practice today — that root is honored.
verses.background: The chapter closes on the hiddenness and the disclosure: the unintelligent take the unmanifest to have become merely visible; veiled by yoga-maya He is not revealed to all; He knows all beings, none know Him — yet those freed of the delusion of opposites worship Him firmly and know Him even at the hour of departure.

verses.devanagari:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः ।
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः॥
verses.transliteration:
sādhibhūtādhidaivaṃ māṃ sādhiyajñaṃ ca ye viduḥ
prayāṇakāle'pi ca māṃ te viduryuktacetasaḥ
verses.essence: Those who know me together with the adhibhuta, the adhidaiva and the adhiyajna — they, of harnessed minds, know me even at the hour of departure.
verses.translation: Those who know Me with the Adhibhuta (pertaining to the elements), Adhidaiva (pertaining to the gods) and the Adhiyajna (pertaining to the sacrifice) know Me even at the time of death, steadfast in mind.

anvaya.meaning: me, with the adhibhuta and adhidaiva
and with the adhiyajna, those who know
even at the time of departure, me
they know, of yoked minds

verses.explanation: The chapter closes mid-sentence, deliberately: three technical terms dropped without definition, and one phrase that changes the conversation's gravity — prayāṇa-kāle, at the time of going forth. To know him so thoroughly that even dying does not dislodge the knowing: that is the final examination, and chapter 8 will be its study guide.
verses.connection: The cliffhanger that generates chapter 8: Arjuna's next words will be exactly these undefined terms.
verses.reflection: Hold one truth firmly enough today that you could hold it at the hour of departure.
verses.background: The chapter closes on the hiddenness and the disclosure: the unintelligent take the unmanifest to have become merely visible; veiled by yoga-maya He is not revealed to all; He knows all beings, none know Him — yet those freed of the delusion of opposites worship Him firmly and know Him even at the hour of departure.
